title: Production Agent Loop and Goal Capstone · Incident Triage
summary: Build a safe goal-driven agent for a realistic repository or operations task. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a production-ready agent-loop reference implementation and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose workflow or agent patterns and justify complexity from task uncertainty.
Connect persistent goals, adaptive plans, tool state, and terminal conditions.
Integrate permissions, idempotency, stop controls, and fault recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:

The record names the assumption, applies Architecture Choice, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: loops-operations-defense-157eq6" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Operations Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: loops-operations-defense-157eq6
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Operations Defense applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend architecture, limitations, cost, monitoring, and operator procedures.
